question: Will the weekly dollar amount of loans made under the primary credit lending program by the Federal Reserve have increased by each resolution date listed below as compared to its value on 2025-03-30?
resolution_criteria:
Resolves to the value found at https://fred.stlouisfed.org/series/WLCFLPCL once the data is published.

The exact value on 2025-03-30 is unknown, but the most recent known value (as of 2025-03-20) was 2417.000. (The latest value released in Assets: Liquidity and Credit Facilities: Loans: Primary Credit: Wednesday Level from the release H.4.1 Factors Affecting Reserve Balances.)
background:
The notes from the release: For questions on the data, please contact the data source: https://www.federalreserve.gov/apps/ContactUs/feedback.aspx?refurl=/releases/h41/%
For questions on FRED functionality, please contact: https://fred.stlouisfed.org/contactus/.  The notes from the series: Primary credit is a lending program available to depository institutions that are in generally sound financial condition. Primary credit is available in terms from overnight to 28 days. In extending primary credit, Reserve Banks must judge that the borrower is likely to remain eligible for primary credit for the term of the loan..  Additional background of the series:  1. the units of the series: Millions of U.S. Dollars.  2. the seasonal adjustments of the series:  Not Seasonally Adjusted  3. the update frequency: Weekly, As of Wednesday
